title: n8n vs Zapier vs Make - honest comparison
body:
Tested all three extensively:

n8n:
+ Self-hosted, full control
+ 400+ integrations
- Steeper learning curve

Zapier:
+ Easiest to start
- Expensive at scale

Make:
+ Visual builder
+ Good free tier

My verdict: n8n for developers, Make for businesses.

What's your experience?

title: Docker security best practices - checklist
body:
Security checklist for Docker:

- Run as non-root user
- Use multi-stage builds
- Scan images for vulnerabilities
- Don't store secrets in images
- Use .dockerignore
- Limit resources
- Read-only filesystem

Full guide on my GitHub.
